products.title: Imperial Shuttle - Loose - 1984
products.body_html: The Imperial Shuttle from 1984 is a classic collectible that captures the iconic Star Wars spacecraft in vintage form. This loose example retains the distinctive angular design and detailed cockpit features characteristic of the original release.

products.title: Imperial TIE Fighter - 1976 - White
products.body_html: The iconic Imperial TIE Fighter from 1976 brings classic Star Wars starfighter design to your collection.

products.title: Rancor Monster 2008 - Loose
products.body_html: The fearsome Rancor from Star Wars—Jabba the Hutt's most terrifying beast. This 2008 loose figure captures the creature's massive, muscular form with impressive detail and articulation. Perfect for collectors recreating iconic Tatooine scenes or expanding their Star Wars collection. A must-have for fans of the original trilogy and Jabba's palace storyline.

products.title: Sandtrooper - Militaries of Star Wars
products.body_html:
Sandtrooper - Militaries of Star Wars Elite desert combat specialist from the Star Wars universe, this Sandtrooper figure captures the iconic armor and tactical gear of Tatooine's Imperial forces.

 Featuring detailed sculpting, authentic paint applications, and articulation for dynamic posing, it's an essential addition for Star Wars collectors and fans of the original trilogy.

Sealed, like new

products.title: Slave One - Loose
products.body_html: This loose Kenner Slave One is the legendary starship of bounty hunter Boba Fett from the original Star Wars toy line. Featuring the classic 1980s design with its recognizable angular profile, opening cockpit, and detailed molding, it's a standout piece for vintage collectors. The vehicle pairs naturally with Kenner figures and displays beautifully as a centerpiece of any classic Star Wars collection.

products.title: Sonic Screwdriver - Trans-Temporal 2
products.body_html:
Nate Grant's sonic screwdriver, also known as the trans-temporal sonic screwdriver, was a sonic screwdriver constructed by Nate Grant that functioned in a similar fashion to the Doctor's sonic screwdriver. Aside from being a tool, the sonic screwdriver could be used and considered as a defensive weapon.
